title: hex_encode builds String via per-byte format! allocations
description:
LOCATION: src/did.rs:121

FINDING: hex_encode is implemented as bytes.iter().map(|b| format!("{b:02x}")).collect::<String>(). Each format! call allocates a temporary String of 2 chars before collecting. For the 32-byte recovery pubkey this produces 32 temporary 2-char heap allocations plus one final collect, where a single pre-allocated buffer with write! would do the same work in a single allocation.

IMPACT: Only called during persona creation and DID backfill (not a hot path), so no runtime impact. However, the pattern is misleading as a model for other low-level encoding work — if copied, it will cause allocation churn elsewhere. The fix is one line: use a pre-allocated String with write! in a loop or hex crate.
close_reason: Fixed: hex_encode uses pre-allocated String with fmt::Write
